QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_17

question.text: Under the PMLA 2002, the punishment for money laundering is:

answer.text: Rigorous imprisonment of 3 to 7 years and fine; up to 10 years for specified offences
Simple imprisonment up to 3 years only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 4 of PMLA prescribes rigorous imprisonment of not less than 3 years but up to 7 years, plus fine. For offences under paragraph 2 of Part A of the Schedule (specified serious offences), imprisonment may extend to 10 years. **The Trap:** The imprisonment is 'rigorous' (with hard labour), not 'simple'. Also, the fine is in ADDITION to imprisonment, not in lieu of it. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV (AML/CFT) and IFSCA-01 cover PMLA provisions extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PMLA punishment: minimum 3 years rigorous imprisonment + fine. Maximum 7 years (general) or 10 years (Schedule Part A paragraph 2 offences). Burden of proof is on the ACCUSED (Section 24) — reverse burden.
